slides.label: Overview
slides.title: Service Performance
slides.prose:
A breakdown of production performance metrics across all backend services. Three key dimensions — response time, throughput, and error rate — tell the story of system health.

Each metric reveals different bottlenecks. A service can be fast but fragile (low latency, high errors) or slow but reliable (high latency, zero errors).

Navigate through each metric to see where the system excels and where it struggles.

slides.label: Latency
slides.title: Response Time
slides.prose:
Response time measures the P95 latency for each service. The Upload service is the slowest at 340ms — expected for large file operations.

The CDN layer is the fastest at 12ms, serving cached static assets from the edge. Most user-facing requests never hit the origin server.

The SSR pipeline at 210ms is the main optimization target — server-side rendering of markdown with emberDiagrams diagrams is CPU-intensive.

slides.label: Throughput
slides.title: Requests per Second
slides.prose:
Throughput shows the sustained request capacity per service. The CDN leads at 25,000 req/s — edge caching makes this almost free to scale.

The Upload service is the bottleneck at 450 req/s. Each upload involves multipart parsing, virus scanning, and R2 storage writes.

WebSocket connections sustain 12,000 concurrent connections for real-time comment updates and collaboration features.

Search throughput increased 4x after adding a full-text index. The bottleneck shifted from the database to the serialization layer, which we solved with streaming responses.

slides.label: Errors
slides.title: Error Rate
slides.prose:
Error rate tracks the percentage of requests returning 4xx/5xx status codes. Upload and WebSocket are the most error-prone services.

Upload errors (4.2%) are primarily timeouts on large files from slow connections. Chunked upload with retry logic cut this from 8.5% to 4.2%.

WebSocket errors (3.8%) are mostly reconnection failures when clients switch networks. Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff is in progress.

Upload errors halved after implementing chunked uploads with automatic retry. Timeout-related 504s on large files dropped to near zero.
